channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Smithsonian too focused on 'oppressors and the oppressed,' say House Republicans
item.description: In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives excoriated the director of the National Museum of American History, following a White House report earlier this month that also criticized her.
item.content:encoded:
In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives excoriated the director of the National Museum of American History, following a White House report earlier this month that also criticized her.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Broadway legend André De Shields helps bring 'Hadestown' to the big screen
item.description: De Shields made his Broadway debut in the 1975 original production of The Wiz. He won a Tony in 2019 for his performance in Hadestown and co-stars in the new film of last year's London revival.
item.content:encoded: De Shields made his Broadway debut in the 1975 original production of The Wiz. He won a Tony in 2019 for his performance in Hadestown and co-stars in the new film of last year's London revival.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: The best games of 2026 so far, picked by NPR's staff
item.description: Titans like Resident Evil Requiem and Crimson Desert dominated in the first half of the gaming year, with early-access sequels like Slay the Spire 2 and Subnautica 2 not far behind.
item.content:encoded: Titans like Resident Evil Requiem and Crimson Desert dominated in the first half of the gaming year, with early-access sequels like Slay the Spire 2 and Subnautica 2 not far behind.
item.dc:creator: James Perkins Mastromarino

item.title: A teen was murdered in 1985. A new book tracks the case through a broken legal system
item.description: Catch the Devil by journalist Pamela Colloff highlights the potentially fatal consequences of a system more concerned with securing convictions than determining the truth and delivering justice.
item.content:encoded: Catch the Devil by journalist Pamela Colloff highlights the potentially fatal consequences of a system more concerned with securing convictions than determining the truth and delivering justice.
item.dc:creator: Kristen Martin

item.title: How soccer brings a Bhutanese community together in Harrisburg, Pa.
item.description: Harrisburg is home to one of the nation's largest communities of Nepali-speaking Bhutanese refugees and their families. Through soccer, generations have found connection, belonging and a way to carry forward a shared history.
item.content:encoded:
Harrisburg is home to one of the nation's largest communities of Nepali-speaking Bhutanese refugees and their families. Through soccer, generations have found connection, belonging and a way to carry forward a shared history.

(Image credit: Maansi Srivastava)
item.dc:creator: Maansi Srivastava

item.title: At Mississippi funeral, civil rights leaders call for justice for Nolan Wells, 18
item.description: The parents of Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi, held a funeral for the 18-year-old on Monday. Wells was with friends for the Fourth of July and did not return home.
item.content:encoded:
The parents of Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi, held a funeral for the 18-year-old on Monday. Wells was with friends for the Fourth of July and did not return home.

(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)
item.dc:creator: Elise Gregg

item.title: How a teacher's feedback changed things for a stuttering student
item.description: In high school, Susan Greenstein Prescott had a stutter and was embarrassed to speak in front of her class. Then her English teacher told her something that changed how she saw herself.
item.content:encoded:
In high school, Susan Greenstein Prescott had a stutter and was embarrassed to speak in front of her class. Then her English teacher told her something that changed how she saw herself.

(Image credit: Greenstein Prescott family photo)
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes

item.title: A cavalcade of international stars headlined the World Cup's first-ever halftime final show
item.description: The 11-minute extravaganza during Sunday's final game featured Justin Bieber, Madonna, Shakira, Burna Boy, Gustavo Dudamel and Coldplay. We have the post-show analysis.
item.content:encoded: The 11-minute extravaganza during Sunday's final game featured Justin Bieber, Madonna, Shakira, Burna Boy, Gustavo Dudamel and Coldplay. We have the post-show analysis.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Court ruling freezes Paramount-Warner Bros. merger for now
item.description: Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.
item.content:encoded:
Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.

(Image credit: SOPA Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Harriet Clark's novel 'The Hill' draws on her 37 years visiting her mom in prison
item.description: Clark was a baby in 1981 when her mother, a member of a radical left-wing group, acted as a getaway driver in the robbery of a Brink's truck. Her new novel is based on the years that followed.
item.content:encoded: Clark was a baby in 1981 when her mother, a member of a radical left-wing group, acted as a getaway driver in the robbery of a Brink's truck. Her new novel is based on the years that followed.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross
